{
  "verdict": "PASS",
  "artifact_sha256": "c6dd3c8ae4da342592dab0814a9f1020288fa0e2a3dd17806532c286306d627a",
  "summary": "Plan-only artifact adequately specifies a hermetic, deterministic config-loader release check covering strict JSON, closed-schema unknown-field rejection, exact frontier model IDs, plaintext-secret rejection, env key-name preservation, and unambiguous pass/fail emission, without claiming workspace access or completed execution.",
  "criteria_reviewed": [
    "Plan-only mode: no workspace access or execution claims required or made as completed work",
    "Deterministic pure-function / CLI contract with stable outputs and exit codes",
    "Accept valid strict JSON (object root, RFC-style constraints)",
    "Exact frontier model ID enforcement (required grok-4.5; optional openai/gpt-5.6-sol only with disabled posture)",
    "Unknown-field rejection via frozen closed schema / additionalProperties false at all depths",
    "Plaintext-secret rejection (unconditional denylist; env refs allowed)",
    "Environment-variable key-name preservation (verbatim, no expansion)",
    "Unambiguous machine-primary pass/fail result shape and release rule",
    "Failure handling, flake/determinism controls, and amendment-on-drift posture",
    "Verification design: golden PASS/FAIL vectors, mutation, determinism, independence (explicitly pending)",
    "Explicit external inputs called out (schema, env syntax, disabled marker, secret ruleset) without inventing full field lists",
    "Safety: no network, no env expansion, no config execution; adversarial values only validated",
    "Alignment with supplied mechanical evidence as non-sufficient background only",
    "Conciseness vs completeness tradeoff for a smoke release-check plan"
  ],
  "blind_spots": [],
  "blocking_findings": [],
  "non_blocking_findings": [
    "Artifact is thorough rather than minimal; still within an acceptable 'concise plan' bound given explicit assumptions, edge cases, and pending verification checklist.",
    "Optional tighter GPT reject-if-present profile and fingerprint/hermetic-walker hardenings are correctly marked optional and do not alter the default PASS set."
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "Original goal explicitly defines plan-only smoke problem and forbids workspace/execution claims.",
    "Candidate states pure specification, no workspace access, loader execution, network I/O, or env expansion; all runtime verification items labeled pending.",
    "Ordered checks 1–6 map directly to accept JSON, exact model IDs, unknown fields, plaintext secrets, env key preservation, and unambiguous PASS/FAIL.",
    "Result contract ties status/errors/exit code with sorted stable rule IDs; ship only on PASS.",
    "Assumptions section refuses to invent full schema/env-syntax/disabled-marker/secret-denylist and requires frozen external artifacts.",
    "Mechanical evidence (MCP doctor OK/13 tools, 166 tests passed, validation_errors=[]) is cited only as baseline background and explicitly non-sufficient for secret/unknown-field proof.",
    "Edge cases, failure handling, implementer checklist, and pre-execution verification plan provide actionability without asserting completed runs."
  ],
  "required_actions": []
}

criteria_reviewed: Plan-only mode: no workspace access or execution claims required or made as completed work
Deterministic pure-function / CLI contract with stable outputs and exit codes
Accept valid strict JSON (object root, RFC-style constraints)
Exact frontier model ID enforcement (required grok-4.5; optional openai/gpt-5.6-sol only with disabled posture)
Unknown-field rejection via frozen closed schema / additionalProperties false at all depths
Plaintext-secret rejection (unconditional denylist; env refs allowed)
Environment-variable key-name preservation (verbatim, no expansion)
Unambiguous machine-primary pass/fail result shape and release rule
Failure handling, flake/determinism controls, and amendment-on-drift posture
Verification design: golden PASS/FAIL vectors, mutation, determinism, independence (explicitly pending)
Explicit external inputs called out (schema, env syntax, disabled marker, secret ruleset) without inventing full field lists
Safety: no network, no env expansion, no config execution; adversarial values only validated
Alignment with supplied mechanical evidence as non-sufficient background only
Conciseness vs completeness tradeoff for a smoke release-check plan
evidence: Original goal explicitly defines plan-only smoke problem and forbids workspace/execution claims.
Candidate states pure specification, no workspace access, loader execution, network I/O, or env expansion; all runtime verification items labeled pending.
Ordered checks 1–6 map directly to accept JSON, exact model IDs, unknown fields, plaintext secrets, env key preservation, and unambiguous PASS/FAIL.
Result contract ties status/errors/exit code with sorted stable rule IDs; ship only on PASS.
Assumptions section refuses to invent full schema/env-syntax/disabled-marker/secret-denylist and requires frozen external artifacts.
Mechanical evidence (MCP doctor OK/13 tools, 166 tests passed, validation_errors=[]) is cited only as baseline background and explicitly non-sufficient for secret/unknown-field proof.
Edge cases, failure handling, implementer checklist, and pre-execution verification plan provide actionability without asserting completed runs.
non_blocking_findings: Artifact is thorough rather than minimal; still within an acceptable 'concise plan' bound given explicit assumptions, edge cases, and pending verification checklist.
Optional tighter GPT reject-if-present profile and fingerprint/hermetic-walker hardenings are correctly marked optional and do not alter the default PASS set.
verdict.summary: Plan-only artifact adequately specifies a hermetic, deterministic config-loader release check covering strict JSON, closed-schema unknown-field rejection, exact frontier model IDs, plaintext-secret rejection, env key-name preservation, and unambiguous pass/fail emission, without claiming workspace access or completed execution.

response.text:
{
  "verdict": "PASS",
  "artifact_sha256": "c6dd3c8ae4da342592dab0814a9f1020288fa0e2a3dd17806532c286306d627a",
  "summary": "Plan-only artifact adequately specifies a hermetic, deterministic config-loader release check covering strict JSON, closed-schema unknown-field rejection, exact model-ID policy, plaintext-secret rejection, env key-name preservation, and unambiguous pass/fail emission, without claiming workspace access or completed execution.",
  "criteria_reviewed": [
    "Plan-only mode: no workspace access or execution claims",
    "Deterministic pure-function / exit-code contract",
    "Accept valid strict JSON (object root)",
    "Reject unknown fields (additionalProperties false / closed schema)",
    "Exact frontier model ID enforcement with amendment control",
    "Plaintext secret rejection (unconditional on release gate)",
    "Environment-variable key-name preservation (no rewrite/expansion)",
    "Unambiguous machine-primary pass/fail result shape and exit codes",
    "Ordered checks and PASS/FAIL summary table completeness",
    "Failure handling, flake/determinism, and fail-closed drift policy",
    "Verification/golden-vector plan present and marked pending",
    "Edge-case coverage for ID confusables, secrets vs env refs, unknown nested fields",
    "Safety: no config execution, no env expansion, no network I/O in the check",
    "Alignment with supplied mechanical evidence (background only, not sole pass signal)",
    "Conciseness relative to stated goal wording",
    "Explicit assumptions for missing schema/env-syntax/secret-ruleset inputs"
  ],
  "blind_spots": [],
  "blocking_findings": [],
  "non_blocking_findings": [
    "Document is long versus the goal’s “concise” wording; core contract could be shorter while retaining checklists as appendix material.",
    "Full closed schema, env-reference syntax, disabled-marker spelling, and secret denylist remain required external frozen inputs—appropriate honesty for plan-only, but the gate is not runnable until they are supplied.",
    "Specific model IDs (grok-4.5, openai/gpt-5.6-sol) and seat posture are pinned from narrative context beyond the three-line mechanical evidence fence; amendment control mitigates drift risk.",
    "Pattern-based secret denylist is acknowledged as inherently partial (false negatives/positives); residual detection risk is owned by the external ruleset."
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "Original goal explicitly frames a plan-only smoke problem and forbids workspace/execution claims; candidate labels itself pure specification and marks all runtime verification pending.",
    "Checks 1–6 map 1:1 to goal mandates: strict JSON, unknown-field rejection, exact model IDs, plaintext-secret reject, env key preservation, unambiguous PASS/FAIL.",
    "Result contract requires status/errors/exit-code consistency (errors empty iff pass; exit 0 iff pass) and sorted stable rule IDs.",
    "Candidate cites mechanical evidence (MCP doctor 13 tools, 166 tests passed, validation_errors=[]) only as non-sufficient background and forbids treating it as sole pass signal.",
    "Edge-case and failure-handling sections specify fail-closed behavior for ID variants, unknown fields, plaintext secrets, env renames, and schema/ID drift.",
    "Implementer checklist and pending golden-vector/determinism/mutation plan provide actionability without asserting completed execution."
  ],
  "required_actions": []
}

criteria_reviewed: Plan-only mode: no workspace access or execution claims
Deterministic pure-function / exit-code contract
Accept valid strict JSON (object root)
Reject unknown fields (additionalProperties false / closed schema)
Exact frontier model ID enforcement with amendment control
Plaintext secret rejection (unconditional on release gate)
Environment-variable key-name preservation (no rewrite/expansion)
Unambiguous machine-primary pass/fail result shape and exit codes
Ordered checks and PASS/FAIL summary table completeness
Failure handling, flake/determinism, and fail-closed drift policy
Verification/golden-vector plan present and marked pending
Edge-case coverage for ID confusables, secrets vs env refs, unknown nested fields
Safety: no config execution, no env expansion, no network I/O in the check
Alignment with supplied mechanical evidence (background only, not sole pass signal)
Conciseness relative to stated goal wording
Explicit assumptions for missing schema/env-syntax/secret-ruleset inputs
evidence: Original goal explicitly frames a plan-only smoke problem and forbids workspace/execution claims; candidate labels itself pure specification and marks all runtime verification pending.
Checks 1–6 map 1:1 to goal mandates: strict JSON, unknown-field rejection, exact model IDs, plaintext-secret reject, env key preservation, unambiguous PASS/FAIL.
Result contract requires status/errors/exit-code consistency (errors empty iff pass; exit 0 iff pass) and sorted stable rule IDs.
Candidate cites mechanical evidence (MCP doctor 13 tools, 166 tests passed, validation_errors=[]) only as non-sufficient background and forbids treating it as sole pass signal.
Edge-case and failure-handling sections specify fail-closed behavior for ID variants, unknown fields, plaintext secrets, env renames, and schema/ID drift.
Implementer checklist and pending golden-vector/determinism/mutation plan provide actionability without asserting completed execution.
non_blocking_findings: Document is long versus the goal’s “concise” wording; core contract could be shorter while retaining checklists as appendix material.

Full closed schema, env-reference syntax, disabled-marker spelling, and secret denylist remain required external frozen inputs—appropriate honesty for plan-only, but the gate is not runnable until they are supplied.
Specific model IDs (grok-4.5, openai/gpt-5.6-sol) and seat posture are pinned from narrative context beyond the three-line mechanical evidence fence; amendment control mitigates drift risk.
Pattern-based secret denylist is acknowledged as inherently partial (false negatives/positives); residual detection risk is owned by the external ruleset.
verdict.summary: Plan-only artifact adequately specifies a hermetic, deterministic config-loader release check covering strict JSON, closed-schema unknown-field rejection, exact model-ID policy, plaintext-secret rejection, env key-name preservation, and unambiguous pass/fail emission, without claiming workspace access or completed execution.
